---
title: "Python Package"
toc: true
---

The `repos` Python package wraps the same Bash scripts used by the command-line
tool, so every feature available at the terminal is also available from Python.

## Installation

```bash
pip install git+https://github.com/MiguelRodo/repos.git
```

Or from a local clone:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/MiguelRodo/repos.git
cd repos
pip install .
```

**System requirements:** `bash`, `git`, `curl`, and `jq` must be available on
your `PATH`.  On Windows, use [Git for Windows](https://git-scm.com/download/win)
or WSL.

## End-to-End Workflow

### Step 1 — Create a `repos.list` file

Place a `repos.list` file in your project directory listing the repositories
you want to manage (one per line):

```
myorg/data-curation
myorg/analysis
myorg/documentation
```

See the [repos.list reference](repos-list.html) for the full syntax — branch
pinning, worktrees, visibility flags, and more.

### Step 2 — Clone and configure repositories

```python
from repos import setup

setup()
```

This reads `repos.list`, creates any missing GitHub repositories, clones
everything to the **parent directory** of your current location, and generates a
VS Code workspace file.  See [`setup()`](#setup) below for all options.

### Step 3 — Run a pipeline

Once the repositories are cloned, run a script inside each one:

```python
from repos import run

run()          # executes run.sh in every repo
```

See [`run()`](#run) below for all options.

## API Reference

### `setup()` {#setup}

Clone and configure repositories from a `repos.list` file.

```python
from repos import setup

setup(
    file=None,            # path to a custom list file (default: repos.list)
    public=False,         # create new repos as public
    codespaces=False,     # configure Codespaces authentication
    devcontainer=None,    # path(s) to devcontainer.json (implies codespaces=True)
    permissions=None,     # "all" or "contents" — passed to codespaces-auth-add.sh
    tool=None,            # force tool for codespaces-auth-add.sh ("jq" or "python")
    debug=False,          # write debug output to stderr
    debug_file=None,      # write debug output to a file (True = auto-name, or pass a path)
)
```

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `file` | `str` | `None` | Path to repos list file (default: `repos.list`) |
| `public` | `bool` | `False` | Create new repositories as public |
| `codespaces` | `bool` | `False` | Enable Codespaces authentication |
| `devcontainer` | `str` or `list[str]` | `None` | Path(s) to `devcontainer.json` |
| `permissions` | `str` | `None` | `"all"` or `"contents"` |
| `tool` | `str` | `None` | Force `"jq"` or `"python"` for auth helper |
| `debug` | `bool` | `False` | Print debug output to stderr |
| `debug_file` | `bool` or `str` | `None` | Write debug output to a file |

**Returns:** `subprocess.CompletedProcess`

**Examples:**

```python
from repos import setup

# Default — reads repos.list in the current directory
setup()

# Use a custom list file
setup(file="my-repos.list")

# Create new repositories as public
setup(public=True)

# Enable Codespaces auth
setup(codespaces=True)

# Specify devcontainer paths (also enables Codespaces auth)
setup(devcontainer=[".devcontainer/devcontainer.json"])

# Combine options
setup(public=True, codespaces=True, debug=True)
```

For the full behavioural description, see the [repos setup guide](setup.html).

### `run()` {#run}

Execute a script inside each cloned repository.

```python
from repos import run

run(
    file=None,              # path to a custom list file (default: repos.list)
    script=None,            # script to run in each repo (default: run.sh)
    include=None,           # repo name(s) to include
    exclude=None,           # repo name(s) to skip
    ensure_setup=False,     # run setup() before executing scripts
    skip_deps=False,        # skip install-r-deps.sh
    dry_run=False,          # print what would run without executing
    verbose=False,          # verbose logging
    continue_on_error=False # keep running after a failure
)
```

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `file` | `str` | `None` | Path to repos list file (default: `repos.list`) |
| `script` | `str` | `None` | Script file to run in each repo (default: `run.sh`) |
| `include` | `str` or `list[str]` | `None` | Only run in these repos |
| `exclude` | `str` or `list[str]` | `None` | Skip these repos |
| `ensure_setup` | `bool` | `False` | Run `setup()` first |
| `skip_deps` | `bool` | `False` | Skip R dependency installation |
| `dry_run` | `bool` | `False` | Preview without executing |
| `verbose` | `bool` | `False` | Verbose logging |
| `continue_on_error` | `bool` | `False` | Continue past failures |

**Returns:** `subprocess.CompletedProcess`

**Examples:**

```python
from repos import run

# Run the default script (run.sh) in every repo
run()

# Run a custom script
run(script="build.sh")

# Only run in specific repos
run(include=["backend", "frontend"])

# Skip a repo
run(exclude="docs")

# Continue even if one repo fails
run(continue_on_error=True)

# Preview without running
run(dry_run=True)

# Ensure repos are cloned before running
run(ensure_setup=True)

# Combine options
run(script="test.sh", verbose=True, ensure_setup=True)
```

For the full behavioural description, see [Running Pipelines](pipelines.html).

### Raw argument passing

If you need to pass flags not yet exposed as keyword arguments, use the raw
helpers:

```python
from repos import setup_raw, run_raw

setup_raw("--public", "--codespaces")
setup_raw("-f", "custom.list", "--public")

run_raw("--script", "build.sh", "--dry-run")
run_raw("-f", "custom.list", "--continue-on-error")
```

These functions forward all positional arguments directly to the underlying
Bash script without any processing.

## CLI Usage

Installing the Python package also registers a `repos` command that delegates
directly to the same Bash scripts:

```bash
repos setup
repos setup --public
repos run
repos run --script build.sh
repos run --include "backend,frontend"
```

This is the same interface documented in the [repos setup guide](setup.html) and
[Running Pipelines](pipelines.html).

## GitHub Authentication

`setup()` needs a GitHub token to create repositories and clone private ones.
Provide it via the `GH_TOKEN` environment variable or via the `gh` CLI:

```python
import os, subprocess
from repos import setup

os.environ["GH_TOKEN"] = "your_personal_access_token"
setup()
```

Or in a shell before starting Python:

```bash
export GH_TOKEN="your_personal_access_token"
python my_script.py
```
